vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inconsistent with visual reality. The pots contain soil and debris, but no identifiable foliage corresponding to the P1-P4 registry. The 'White Rabbit' anchor is absent from the current frame.

plant_audit.observations: All pots appear to be empty of living plant matter. The soil surface is dark and appears to contain only inorganic debris (white fragments, possibly perlite or eggshell) and a sensor/probe in the yellow pot.

biome_observations.debris: Presence of white particulate matter (likely perlite) and a sensor probe in the yellow pot. A cylindrical object (possibly a nutrient stick or marker) is present in the yellow pot.
biome_observations.anomalies: Total absence of expected botanical specimens. The environment appears to be a dormant or failed propagation setup.

temporal_deltas.T_minus_5_to_Current: No significant growth or change observed. The pots have remained in a state of stasis with no emergence of plant life throughout the 5-day observation window.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. There is no evidence of photosynthesis or active plant growth. The system is effectively a 'dead' or 'empty' soil environment.

vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image sequence. My first step was to establish the baseline registry and compare it against the visual data. Upon validation, it is clear that the expected plants (P1-P4) are not present in any of the images. The pots contain only soil and inert materials. There has been no change in the status of the pots over the 5-day period; they are consistently devoid of vegetation. The environment is currently a failed or dormant botanical setup.
